problem: The Rotokas of Papua New Guinea have twelve letters in their alphabet. The letters are: A, E, G, I, K, O, P, R, S, T, U, and V. Suppose license plates of five letters utilize only the letters in the Rotoka alphabet. How many license plates of five letters are possible that begin with either G or K, end with T, cannot contain S, and have no letters that repeat?

classified_questions.question: How many letters are there in the Rotokas alphabet?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the letters in the Rotokas alphabet?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many letters can be chosen for the first position in the license plate?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many letters can be chosen for the last position in the license plate?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why must the last letter be chosen as T?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can the letters S and T appear in the license plate? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b
